article_title: Learn Hardware, Cloud, DevOps, Networking, Security, Databases, DNS, Git, and Linux
core_thesis: freeCodeCamp has released a new 13-hour video course titled 'Ultimate IT Fundamentals Bootcamp For Absolute Beginners' by DolfinED Academy, designed to provide a comprehensive introduction to core IT topics including hardware, cloud computing, DevOps, networking, security, databases, DNS, Git, and Linux, with hands-on practice in Docker and containerization.
detailed_summary: The article announces a free, full-length video course available on the freeCodeCamp YouTube channel. The course is aimed at absolute beginners and covers a wide range of IT fundamentals: hardware basics, cloud technologies, DevOps principles, networking concepts, security essentials, databases, DNS, Git version control, and Linux operating system. It also includes an introduction to containerization with Docker. The course is 13 hours long and is created by DolfinED Academy. The article emphasizes that the course provides a complete toolkit to kickstart an IT career, and it is part of freeCodeCamp's mission to offer free coding education.

entities.name: freeCodeCamp
entities.role: Non-profit organization that provides free coding education and hosts the course on its YouTube channel.

entities.name: DolfinED Academy
entities.role: Creator of the 'Ultimate IT Fundamentals Bootcamp For Absolute Beginners' course.

entities.name: Beau Carnes
entities.role: Teacher and developer at freeCodeCamp who wrote the article and runs the freeCodeCamp YouTube channel.

key_data_points: The course is 13 hours long and covers hardware, cloud, DevOps, networking, security, databases, DNS, Git, and Linux (🟢 relevant to your Red Teaming: foundational knowledge of networking, security, and Linux is essential for offensive security operations).

The course includes hands-on practice with Docker and containerization (🟢 relevant to your DevOps/homelab interests: Docker is a key tool for deploying and isolating applications in a homelab environment).

The course is designed for absolute beginners, making it a suitable starting point for building a broad IT skill set (🟢 relevant to your student needs: provides a structured, free resource to fill knowledge gaps).
conclusions: The freeCodeCamp course offers a comprehensive, free, and structured introduction to IT fundamentals, which can serve as a solid foundation for further specialization in cybersecurity, DevOps, or cloud engineering.
For a Red Teamer, the networking, security, and Linux sections are directly applicable, while Docker and cloud knowledge support modern attack surface understanding.
The course's beginner-friendly approach makes it accessible even if you have no prior IT experience, and the 13-hour length allows for deep dives into each topic.
personal_relevance_summary: This article is highly relevant to your interests as it provides a free, comprehensive IT fundamentals course that covers networking, security, Linux, and DevOps—all core areas for Red Teaming and homelabbing. As a programmer and student, you can use this course to fill gaps in your knowledge, especially in networking and security, which are critical for offensive security. The hands-on Docker content also aligns with your DevOps interests. The course is a time-efficient way to build a broad foundation without cost.
extra_advice: Consider watching the networking and security sections first, as they directly support Red Teaming skills. Then, use the Linux and Docker sections to set up a homelab environment for practicing attacks and defenses. The course can also serve as a reference for study groups or teaching others.
